Ochna pygmaea is a plant species within the family Ochnaceae, known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ochna pygmaea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Ochna pygmaea is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
